questions.question: How do you set default values for props when using the TypeScript generic syntax with defineProps?
options: Add a default property directly inside the TypeScript interface
Pass a second argument to defineProps: defineProps<Props>({ defaults: {...} })
Destructure inside a computed property with fallback values

questions.question: Since Vue 3.5, how do you destructure props while keeping reactivity?
options: Destructure directly from defineProps: reactive destructure is supported natively
Use computed(() => props.title) for each prop individually

questions.question: Why is the TypeScript generic syntax preferred over the runtime object syntax for defineProps?
options: The generic syntax generates faster runtime checks
The runtime syntax doesn't support arrays or objects as prop types
The generic syntax gives full IDE inference, no duplication, and supports complex types
The runtime syntax is deprecated in Vue 3.5+
